tone.notes: Spanish B2B communication places high value on personal rapport and trust before business is discussed; invest time in small talk and relationship-building before diving into proposals. Use 'usted' in initial outreach to show respect, but be prepared to shift to 'tú' once a relationship is established. Avoid overly transactional or cold messaging — warmth, personalisation, and a genuine interest in the prospect's business are essential to open doors.

blackout_periods.name: Año Nuevo (New Year's Day)
blackout_periods.description: 1 January — national public holiday, no business activity.

blackout_periods.name: Epifanía del Señor (Epiphany)
blackout_periods.description: 6 January — major national holiday; offices closed and many businesses wind down from 5 January.

blackout_periods.name: Viernes Santo (Good Friday)
blackout_periods.description: National public holiday; avoid the entire Holy Week (Semana Santa) as many businesses operate at reduced capacity or close entirely, especially in coastal and southern regions.

blackout_periods.name: Semana Santa (Holy Week)
blackout_periods.description: The week leading up to Easter Sunday is a major holiday period in Spain; marina and maritime businesses in coastal areas often pause operations or have skeleton staff.

blackout_periods.name: Fiesta del Trabajo (Labour Day)
blackout_periods.description: 1 May — national public holiday, no business activity.

blackout_periods.name: La Asunción de la Virgen (Assumption of Mary)
blackout_periods.description: 15 August — national public holiday; coincides with peak summer holiday season when many decision-makers are on vacation.

blackout_periods.name: August Summer Holidays
blackout_periods.description: The entire month of August is effectively a low-activity period in Spanish B2B; many businesses close for 2–4 weeks and key decision-makers are unreachable. Avoid major outreach campaigns throughout August.

blackout_periods.name: Fiesta Nacional de España (National Day)
blackout_periods.description: 12 October — national public holiday, no business activity.

blackout_periods.name: Todos los Santos (All Saints' Day)
blackout_periods.description: 1 November — national public holiday; avoid outreach on this day and the surrounding days as many take extended weekends.

blackout_periods.name: Día de la Constitución Española (Constitution Day)
blackout_periods.description: 6 December — national public holiday; often forms a bridge holiday ('puente') with Immaculate Conception on 8 December, creating a multi-day shutdown.

blackout_periods.name: La Inmaculada Concepción (Immaculate Conception)
blackout_periods.description: 8 December — national public holiday; frequently combined with Constitution Day for a long weekend or full week off ('puente de diciembre').

blackout_periods.name: Navidad (Christmas Day)
blackout_periods.description: 25 December — national public holiday; the Christmas and New Year period (24 Dec – 6 Jan) sees very low B2B engagement across Spain.

blackout_periods.name: Christmas–Epiphany Period
blackout_periods.description: 24 December to 6 January — the entire festive period is effectively a blackout for serious B2B outreach; most businesses operate minimally and decisions are deferred until mid-January.

localization.measurement_notes: All measurements should use metric units: boat length in metres (m), depth in metres (m), weight/displacement in kilograms (kg) or tonnes (t), fuel in litres (L), and speed in knots (kn) for maritime context. Berth dimensions should be expressed in metres. Do not use feet or gallons.

compliance.opt_out_language: Si no desea recibir más comunicaciones comerciales de nuestra parte, puede darse de baja en cualquier momento haciendo clic aquí: [Darse de baja]. Respetaremos su solicitud de inmediato.
compliance.required_footer: Este mensaje ha sido enviado por [Nombre de la Empresa], con domicilio social en [Dirección completa], [Ciudad], [Código Postal], España. CIF: [XXXXXXXXX]. Inscrita en el Registro Mercantil de [Ciudad]. Puede ejercer sus derechos de acceso, rectificación, supresión, oposición, limitación del tratamiento y portabilidad de sus datos contactando con nosotros en [email de privacidad] o consultando nuestra Política de Privacidad en [URL]. Responsable del tratamiento: [Nombre de la Empresa].
compliance.notes: Spain is subject to GDPR and has implemented it via the LOPDGDD (Ley Orgánica de Protección de Datos y Garantía de los Derechos Digitales). Commercial electronic communications to businesses are additionally governed by the LSSI-CE, which requires a clear opt-out mechanism in every commercial email. Legitimate interest may be used as a lawful basis for B2B prospecting, but must be documented and balanced against recipient rights. The Spanish data protection authority (AEPD — Agencia Española de Protección de Datos) actively enforces these rules and has issued significant fines. Always include full company details (registered name, address, CIF/NIF tax ID), a clear unsubscribe mechanism, and a link to your privacy policy. Retain records of consent or legitimate interest assessments. Do not send unsolicited commercial emails to individuals without a valid lawful basis.

cultural_notes: Spain has an extensive and vibrant marina and recreational boating industry, particularly along the Mediterranean coast (Costa del Sol, Costa Brava, Balearic Islands, Valencia) and the Atlantic coast (Galicia, Canary Islands), making it one of Europe's most important maritime leisure markets. B2B decisions in Spanish marina businesses tend to involve multiple stakeholders and can move slowly — patience and consistent follow-up are essential, as rushing a deal is seen as disrespectful. Face-to-face meetings, industry events such as the Salón Náutico Internacional de Barcelona, and personal referrals carry significantly more weight than cold digital outreach alone, so whenever possible, seek warm introductions or attend regional maritime trade events to build credibility.
